title: A support system and a method of operating a support system for a belt of a conveyor
abstract: A conveyor belt support system can include two idler roller supports/frames (10, 20). When a roller (11, 12, 13) in a first idler roller frame supporting the belt 50 needs changing, the first idler roller frame (10) rotates away from under the belt. The second idler roller frame (20) rotates to a supporting position replacing the first idler roller frame (10). The second idler roller frame (20) can support in the same position as the first idler roller frame vacated, or can support the belt at an adjacent position. The process can be reversed when the second roller idler frame (20) has a roller needing changing. Movement of the idler roller frames can be actuated by a hydraulic, electrical, mechanical or pneumatic actuator (70). One or more hydraulic rams/cylinders or electrical actuators, such as motors, can be used. The actuator (70) can fitted with a transponder which can send/receive signals through a wired connection or a wireless connection. The signals are communicated with a computer system.
claims: Claims THE CLAIMS DEFINING THE INVENTION ARE AS FOLLOWS: 1 . A support system for a belt of a conveyor including: a first support having a first set of one or more rotary supporting elements for supporting the belt, and a second support having a second set of one or more rotary supporting elements for supporting the belt, wherein said supports are moveable to interchangeably support the belt at a section of the conveyor. 2. The support system of claim 1 , wherein, when one of said supports is moved to a supporting position, the other of said supports is moved to a non- supporting position. 3. The support system of claim 2, wherein the other of said supports is moved to a non-supporting position automatically. 4. The support system of claim 2 or 3, wherein the support in the non- operating position is detachable from the conveyor. 5. The support system of any one of the preceding claims, including a pivot on which said supports are fixedly mounted, and wherein said supports are angularly spaced from each other. 6. The support system of claim 5, wherein the pivot is rotated to move said supports to interchangeably support a section of the belt. 7. The support system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said supports are interchanged when a problem is detected in the support which is supporting the belt. 8. The support system of claim 7, wherein the problem could be detected by monitoring one or more of temperature, vibrations and sound level of the first supporting member. 9. The support system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the support system supports load carrying portion of the belt or return portion of the belt. 10. The support system of any one of the preceding claims, including an actuator to move said supports. 1 1 . The support system of claim 10, wherein the actuator includes an electrical actuator or hydraulic actuator. 12. The support system of claim 1 1 , wherein the hydraulic actuator includes one or multiple hydraulic rams. 13. The support system of claim 12, wherein the hydraulic ram(s) extend to move the first and/or second support in a direction against the direction of motion of the belt. 14. The support system of claim 12 or 13, further including at least one hydraulic fluid flow control valve to restrict retraction and/or extension movement of the hydraulic rams. 15. The support system of any one of claims 10 to 14, wherein the actuator is activated remotely. 16. The support system of any one of claims 10 to 15, including an actuator system unit to power the actuator. 17. The support system of claim 16, wherein the actuator system unit is connected to actuators on other support systems to power the electric actuators or hydraulic rams on such other support systems. 18. A conveyor including a support system according to any one of the preceding claims. 19. The conveyor according to claim 18, including a belt lifting means proximate to the support system for lifting the belt such that it is not supported by said support system to enable movement of said supports when the belt is running. 20. The conveyor according to claim 18 or 19 including a plurality of support systems, wherein the plurality of support systems are spaced apart from each other for supporting the belt at multiple locations. 21 . A method of operating a support system for a belt of a conveyor including the steps of: positioning a first support having a first set of one or more rotary supporting elements and a second support having a second set of one or more rotary supporting elements, proximate to each other; moving the first support and the second support to interchangeably support the belt at a section of the conveyor, at a level. 22. The method of claim 21 including the step of: moving one of said supports from a supporting position to a non-supporting position, and the other of said supports from a non-supporting position to a supporting position. 23. The method of claim 22 including the step of: pivoting said supports. 24. The method of any one of claims 21 to 23 including the step of: lifting a portion of the belt such that said supports do not support the belt, prior to interchanging of said supports. 25. The method of claim 24 including the step of: lowering the belt such that the belt is supported by one of said supports, after interchanging of said supports. 26. The method of any one of claims 21 to 25, including operating one or more hydraulic rams of an actuator system to move the first support and the second support to interchangeably support the belt at a section of the conveyor, the hydraulic ram(s) extending when the first and second supports are moved in a direction against the direction of motion of the belt. 27. The method of claim 26, further including the one or more rams retracting when the first and second supports move in a direction with the direction of motion of the belt. 28. The method of claim 26 or 27, further including operating one or more lock valves to lock hydraulic pressure in the one or more rams when extended. 29. The support system of any one of claims 1 to 17, or the conveyor of any one of claims 18 to 20, or the method of operating the support system of any one claims 21 to 25, wherein movement of said supports is remote controlled. 30. The support system of claim 29, wherein remote controlling of said supports is by means of wired controller or wireless controller such as a handheld remote device. 31 . The support system of claim 29 or 30, wherein remote controlling of said supports is through a computer network such as the internet, local area network or wide area network. 32. The support system of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the first and second supports are mounted to a shared beam arrangement. 33. The support system of claim 32, wherein the shared beam arrangement is pivotably mounted at each respective end thereof.
